description.en: Used for the symbol table of a class.

excerpt.es: Used for the symbol table of a class.
excerpt.en: Used for the symbol table of a class.

description.en: A namespace of a class. This class inherits from SymbolTable. get_methods()¶ Return a tuple containing the names of method-like functions declared in the class. Here, the term ‘method’ designates any function defined in the class body via def or async def. Functions defined in a deeper scope (e.g., in an inner class) are not picked up by get_methods(). For example: >>> import symtable >>> st = symtable.symtable(''' ... def outer(): pass ... ... class A: ... def f(): ... def w(): pass ... ... ...
